response:
the area of the hexagonal community center is found by dividing the regular hexagon into six equilateral triangles each with a side length of 30 meters where the area of one triangle is calculated using the formula one half times the base times the height and the height is derived from the sine of sixty degrees yielding a total area of approximately 2338.27 square meters for the entire structure.

the radius of the circular garden is determined by recognizing that the circle touches the midpoints of the hexagon sides which means the radius equals the apothem of the hexagon calculated as thirty times the cosine of thirty degrees resulting in a radius of approximately 25.98 meters and the area of this circular garden is then computed using the formula pi times the radius squared giving an area of approximately 2120.58 square meters.
